text: The surgeon begins the operation by dissecting the sleeve gastrectomy, carefully dividing adhesions from the omentum to the suture line.
The surgeon carefully dissects the hiatus, passing a Penrose drain around the retrosophageal window. Detailed dissection reveals a previous hernia repair with VLOG suture.
The surgeon dissects the esophagus into the mediastinum, achieving three centimeters of intra-abdominal esophageal length. The hiatus is then closed using two O-etibon sutures.

An intraoperative EGD is performed to inspect the proximal sleeve for kinks or strictures. The surgeon transects the sleeve approximately five centimeters from the gastroesophageal junction, followed by resection of the antrum.
The surgeon performs a jejuno-jejunostomy, followed by a two-layer gastrojejunostomy, creating an 80 cm alimentary limb and a 50 cm biliopancreatic limb.

Following the completion of the Roux-en-Y gastric bypass, the surgeon performs an intraoperative EGD to visually inspect the gastrointestinal tract for any abnormalities or damage. The surgical field shows minimal blood loss.
